rapier.js
=========

Source : @dimforge/rapier3d-compat
Version: 0.19.3
Origin : https://registry.npmjs.org/@dimforge/rapier3d-compat/-/rapier3d-compat-0.19.3.tgz
License: Apache-2.0 (Dimforge SAS)
File   : the package's `rapier.mjs` ESM entry, copied verbatim (unmodified).

Why the -compat build, vendored, unmodified:
  - It inlines the WebAssembly module as a base64 blob inside the JS, so it is
    a single self-contained ES module: no separate .wasm fetch. That is what
    lets it run inside a peerd dwapp's opaque-origin iframe (everything inlined
    at compose time, no relative fetches) AND in Node for the headless
    determinism harness — the same bytes in both places.
  - It is single-threaded (no SharedArrayBuffer), so it needs no COOP/COEP
    cross-origin-isolation headers — again a requirement of the dwapp sandbox.
  - Rapier's wasm build is bit-level cross-platform deterministic (same version
    + same wasm everywhere), which is the property charon's lockstep model and
    replay/determinism harness depend on. See docs/DESIGN-RAPIER-STACK.md.

Usage:
  import RAPIER from '../vendor/rapier.js';
  await RAPIER.init();            // resolves the embedded wasm once
  const world = new RAPIER.World({ x: 0, y: -9.81, z: 0 });

Update procedure:
  npm pack @dimforge/rapier3d-compat@<version>, extract, copy rapier.mjs here,
  bump the version above. Audit the diff before committing (no build step; this
  file ships as-is to users).
